title: Consumer financial services professional
overviewOfRole: Provide financial support and expertise to a range of clients

knowledges.detail: The structure of the financial services industry, including the role of their sector and organisation, and the types of internal organisational roles and structures within financial services.

The features and principles of organisational standards in financial services, and how these are applied within specific contexts, including the features and principles of legal and regulatory frameworks, ethics, impacts, and measurement through Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).

External factors and the wider landscape, such as government budget announcements and economic changes, including approaches to assessing their impact on financial service delivery using contemporary frameworks and approaches such as PESTEL.
Risk and governance frameworks, how these impact their role, approaches to facilitate compliance, and the implications of non-compliance for their organisation.

The role that sustainability policies and practices play in financial services, including approaches to assess positive and negative impacts on environmental sustainability within the context of the role.
Approaches to identifying customer segments, their needs, and service channels relative to their role, including the principles of excellent customer service and stakeholder relationship management.
Fair customer outcomes, including regulatory principles, and organisational standards and values.
Productivity systems, tools, and techniques, such as project management, time management, and prioritisation.
Principles of digital software and tools to support the delivery of financial services, such as data analysis, data visualisation, collaborative working, and communication channels.
Principles of cybersecurity, including risks to financial services from cyber fraud and steps for mitigation.
Principles of safe and secure data access, handling, and storage.
Principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion (EDI) within financial services delivery for stakeholders and within teams.
Approaches to assessing the market, including financial, economic, and market awareness, demographics, and competition.
The range of financial products and services available to stakeholders.
Principles of business dynamics, including how businesses work and develop, such as the business lifecycle and cash flow.
Principles of relationship management and maximising stakeholder value.
Approaches to client portfolio sales, management, and growth.

Approaches to Know Your Customer (KYC) activity, including understanding the macro and micro-environment of each customer, their challenges and motivations, and the internal and external factors that impact their financial decisions.
Principles of financial operations administration to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
Approaches to process mapping and efficiency strategies.
Principles of stress tests or health checks on processes and procedures to ensure that commercial outcomes are compliant and viable.

behaviours.detail: Shows integrity by doing the right thing in adherence to the code of conduct and by demonstrating the organisation’s values.
Maintains confidentiality and acts with due care and diligence.
Demonstrates resilience in challenging or changing environments, adapts positively to shifting work priorities and patterns, and remains flexible to the needs of the organisation.
Is creative, innovative, and enterprising when seeking solutions to business needs, while understanding relevant boundaries.
Is accountable for the quality of work and takes responsibility for decisions and procedures implemented.
Is empathetic towards others and contributes to a diverse and inclusive culture.
Takes ownership of self-development and continuous improvement and supports others in their development.

skills.detail: Build and maintain working relationships with customers and stakeholders, including contracting and managing stakeholder expectations.
Work with stakeholders to identify their relevant financial services requirements and assess associated risks and opportunities.
Implement and maintain finance-related administrative procedures and guidance that enable effective team delivery.
Identify and communicate risks to the relevant management or compliance personnel within the organisation, understanding and following the appropriate processes to address them.
Select and use project management techniques appropriate to the circumstances to enable the delivery of projects or project components.
Contribute to the development of strategic and operational plans for their area.
Report on progress and operational plans to stakeholders, such as senior leaders and team members, using digital tools.
Review, analyse, and evaluate information from different sources to inform decisions when reviewing proposed solutions for clients, customers, or stakeholders.

Communicate financial services concepts to colleagues, customers, and non-technical stakeholders, selecting appropriate communication methods for the audience, such as verbal, written, digital, data visualisations, and presentations.
Lead others within a team or as part of projects to achieve organisational objectives.
Identify opportunities to drive higher performance for self and others, and improve services or processes, for example through the use of digital technologies.
Handle data safely and securely, and share information in compliance with data protection legislation and organisational policy.
Undertake data or financial analysis using digital tools.
Keep up to date with developments in digital technology in financial services relevant to their role, including the associated risks and benefits.
Support the development or implementation of business development or customer contact strategies.

Apply financial analysis techniques relevant to financial services tasks, for example providing guidance on credit appetite, developing practical and appropriate credit solutions or lending structures, or maximising portfolio return.
Identify and provide relevant client market and product information to deliver client-focused, accessible, and ethical information that meets client needs.
Undertake due diligence and risk assessment activities based on a detailed understanding of individual client needs, risk appetite, and exposure to risk.
Support stakeholder account planning and growth activities.
Undertake sales and marketing activities to generate positive commercial outcomes.
Apply compliance processes and procedures to financial operations activities to meet regulatory requirements.

Plan and support the implementation of live changes in financial services operation processes and procedures, including participation in stress tests and health checks to ensure compliance and viability.
Support the provision of feedback to internal stakeholders regarding identified areas for improvement in compliance and operational efficiencies.

involvedEmployers: Barclays, Lloyds, Halifax, The Coventry Building Society, NatWest, HSBC, Metro Bank, Santander, Bank of America, Rathbones, Goldman Sachs, Nationwide, Standard Chartered.
otherInvolvedStakeholders: Kaplan, Walbrook Institute, BPP, Davies Group, Queen Mary University London, Chartered Banker Institute, Fitch learning, Exeter University.
englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Provide high quality advice and support to internal and external clients across a full range of financial instruments and services using specialist knowledge of instruments and services supported and areas of the business worked in

duties.dutyDetail: Support the delivery of financial services through digital or in-person banking solutions to clients or stakeholders, either domestically or globally, by interacting in areas such as payment services, control, change, data, customer experience, and digital initiatives.
Manage operational risks relevant to the role in compliance with applicable internal policies, as well as external laws and regulations.
Deliver financial services activities within the regulatory framework in which the organisation operates, and in compliance with organisational policies and procedures.
Recognise ethical challenges and work within the organisational code of conduct when delivering financial services activities, seeking advice when necessary.
Deliver against individual and team performance measures, such as customer growth and active customer numbers.
Communicate complex financial information using approaches and digital tools tailored to stakeholder needs, to facilitate informed decision-making on financial activities.
Work with stakeholders to develop and deliver initiatives at pace while managing risk and meeting project requirements.
Contribute to identifying areas for improvement within the organisation and follow through until they are implemented.

Carry out activities within the wider financial landscape in line with organisational strategy, such as promoting equity, diversity, and inclusion (EDI), implementing environmental, social, and governance (ESG) policies including green and sustainable finance products, principles, and practices, and upholding high governance standards.
Support others in their professional development by maintaining sector-specific knowledge and sharing it with peers.
Support stakeholders by providing financial guidance and implementing financial solutions.
Handle non-routine stakeholder queries, requests, or complaints, and act as an escalation point for stakeholders.

Represent the organisation with external stakeholders and build contacts to enhance the business’s reputation, for example involvement with customers, clients, or external partners at national and global levels.
Support stakeholders’ financial services needs through analysis and the application of financial modelling and risk management tools.

Plan, launch, and implement new products, services, or processes for stakeholders, and support the provision of financial solutions. These activities may include account planning, developing commercial plans, enhancing client experience, managing risk, structuring and pricing deals, negotiating terms for new debt or agreements, and adhering to pricing policies for debt, credit balances, and money transmission products.
Provide technical industry insights on specialised financial services to deliver appropriate solutions and maximise overall customer satisfaction.
Assist stakeholders in carrying out specialised financial activities and services, such as lending, cash management, and liquidity management.
Address the operational needs and day-to-day transactions of stakeholders, such as supporting routine banking activities or complex corporate financing arrangements.
Drive the sales and growth of stakeholder financial product usage and satisfaction through regular reviews and a solution-based approach, using market and industry information with clients.

Support the management of a portfolio of customer relationships to maximise sustainable income and client profitability, including delivering, monitoring, and reviewing solutions based on financial risk profiles.

Manage operational, reputational, and credit risks for the client portfolio, and adhere to risk service standards, such as collaborating with others to carry out Enhanced Due Diligence reviews for client portfolios.
Deliver market materials to clients, demonstrating in-depth sector and product expertise.
Assist in managing a client book and support the generation of new prospects within a target market.
Manage the administration of financial operations activities to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.

Contribute to ensuring that regulatory, compliance, and commercial changes are promptly and appropriately integrated into live operational processes and procedures to support the effective functioning of the organisation.

Carry out stress tests or health checks on processes and procedures to ensure that commercial outcomes are compliant and viable, for example, perform reconciliations, oversee the performance of control frameworks, and identify any arising operational risks.
Manage the administration of financial operations activities to meet efficiency expectations and drive continuous improvement.

options.title: Retail or commercial financial services professional
